**Overview**

> This documentation is for an unreleased version of Apache Flink Stateful Functions. We recommend you use the latest [stable version](<https://ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-statefun-docs-stable/>).

#  I/O Components #

Stateful Functions' I/O components allow functions to receive and send messages to external systems. Based on the concept of Ingress (input) and Egress (output) points, and built on top of the Apache Flink® connector ecosystem, I/O components enable functions to interact with the outside world through the style of message passing.

Commonly used I/O components are bundled into the runtime by default and can be configured directly via the applications [module configuration](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-statefun-docs-master/docs/modules/overview/>). Additionally, custom connectors for other systems can be [plugged in](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-statefun-docs-master/docs/modules/io/flink-connectors/>) to the runtime.

Remember, to use one of these connectors in an application, third-party components are usually required, e.g., servers for the data stores or message queues.
